sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a history of lower left back pain, which began after back surgery in November 2020, a microdiscectomy due to a herniated disk.
The patient describes the pain as "horrible" and "awful," and states that it has been accompanied by nerve pain.
The patient reports being highly dosed on gabapentin for the pain and experiencing difficulty withdrawing from it.
The patient also reports that stretching helps alleviate the pain, but bending over and weight-bearing activities exacerbate it.
Past medical history: The patient had back surgery in November 2020, a microdiscectomy, and was prescribed gabapentin for pain management.
Review of systems: The patient reports no symptoms in other organs or systems.
Current medications: I prescribed gabapentin for nerve pain, which the patient was taking at a high dose of 800mg per day.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had no pain with flexion or extension, but did experience pain when standing and when palpation was performed on the left side around the L5.
Dorsiflexion was normal, and the patient had normal range of motion.
Test Results: The patient underwent an X-ray, which showed no fractures or abnormalities in the spinal bone, and showed that the area where the patient had surgery had healed properly.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a muscle pull in the left lower back due to a misalignment in the spine.
Problem list: Lower left back pain; muscle pull in left lower back; history of microdiscectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg per day for pain management, and referred the patient to physical therapy.
I also wrote a referral to pain management to discuss alternative pain management options.
I prescribed demerol, but only after discussing the risks of narcotics with the patient.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up in two weeks to assess the effectiveness of the physical therapy and pain management plan.
